item.name: Auth

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Returns the Google OAuth2 URL to redirect the user to for sign-in.

**Frontend flow:**
1. Call this endpoint to get `url` + `state`
2. Store `state` in `sessionStorage`
3. Redirect the browser to `url`
4. After Google redirects back, verify `state` matches and call `/api/auth/callback`

**Returns:** `{ url, state }`

response.name: Get Google OAuth URL

query.description: Authorization code from Google
State value returned by /oauth-url

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Handles the Google OAuth redirect. Google appends `code` and `state` as query params.

**What happens:**
- Backend exchanges `code` for tokens with Google
- Sets `access_token` + `refresh_token` as HttpOnly cookies
- Returns `access_token` and `user` in the response body

**Frontend:** Store `access_token` in memory (Zustand/context — NOT localStorage) and include it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in all subsequent requests.

**Query params:** `code` (required), `state` (required — must match what you stored)

response.name: Google OAuth Callback

query.description: Authorization code from Google
State value returned by /oauth-url

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Authenticate with email and password.

**On success:**
- Returns `access_token` (short-lived JWT) + full `user` object
- Sets `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie (long-lived, sent automatically by browser)

**Frontend flow:**
1. POST `{ email, password }`
2. Store `access_token` in memory (Zustand/Redux — NOT localStorage)
3. Add `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` to all subsequent API calls
4. When you get a 401, call `/api/auth/refresh` to get a new token

response.name: Login
Login

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Create a new user account with email and password.

**Required fields:** `first_name`, `last_name`, `email`, `password`, `confirm_password`
**Optional:** `referral_code` — if provided, links the account to the inviting org

**After registration:**
- A verification email is sent automatically
- `email_verified` will be `false` until the user clicks the link
- If `referral_code` matched an org domain, `domain_match: true` is returned

**Returns 201** with `{ user_id, email_verified, domain_match? }`

response.name: Register
Register

query.description: One-time token from the verification email

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Activate the user's email address using the one-time token sent in the verification email.

**Typical usage:** This URL is the link target in the verification email. After calling this, redirect the user to login.

**Query param:** `token` (required) — from the verification email

response.name: Verify Email

query.description: One-time token from the verification email

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Send a password reset email to the given address.

**Always returns 200** — even if the email doesn't exist (prevents user enumeration).

The email contains a link with a short-lived reset token. Use that token in the `/api/auth/reset-password` endpoint.

response.name: Forgot Password

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Reset the user's password using the token from the reset email.

**Important:** This **invalidates ALL existing JWTs** for the user — all devices are logged out.

**Fields:**
- `token` — the value from the reset link in email
- `new_password` — new password
- `confirm_new_password` — must match `new_password`

response.name: Reset Password

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

Log out the current user.

**What happens:**
- Clears the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie
- Records `last_logout_at` timestamp (invalidates all JWTs issued before this moment)
- All active sessions for this user immediately become invalid

**Frontend:** After calling this, clear `access_token` from memory and redirect to login.

response.name: Logout

header.value: access_token=""; expires=Thu, 12 Mar 2026 08:53:20 GMT; Max-Age=0; Path=/api; SameSite=lax
refresh_token=""; expires=Thu, 12 Mar 2026 08:53:20 GMT; Max-Age=0; Path=/api/auth/refresh; SameSite=lax

response.name: Logout

request.description:
**No Authorization header needed** — relies on the HttpOnly `refresh_token` cookie.

Exchange the cookie for a new `access_token` when the current one expires.

**Frontend pattern:**
1. Make any API call
2. If it returns 401, call this endpoint (browser sends cookie automatically)
3. On success, update `{{authToken}}` and retry the original request
4. If this also returns 401, the session is fully expired — redirect to login

**No request body needed.**

response.name: Refresh Token
Refresh Token

query.description: Invitation token from the email link

request.description:
**No authentication required.**

Validate an organization invitation token from an invite email.

**Response logic:**
- `accepted: false` → The invited email is not registered yet. Use `org_name` and `email` to pre-fill the registration form. After registering (optionally with the referral code), the org join is automatic.
- `accepted: true` → The invited user already had an account and has been added to the org.

**Query param:** `token` (required) — from the invitation link in the email

response.name: Accept Invite (Check Token)

query.description: Invitation token from the email link

response.name: Accept Invite (Check Token)

query.description: Invitation token from the email link

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

Get an OAuth URL to connect an **additional Google account** to the current user's profile (for GA4 / GSC data access — different from the login Google account).

**Frontend flow:**
1. Call this endpoint
2. Store `state` in `sessionStorage`
3. Redirect user to the returned `url`
4. Google will redirect to the backend callback which links the account

**Returns:** `{ url, state }`

response.name: Get Google Account Link URL

query.description: Authorization code from Google
State value from /google-account/oauth-url

request.description:
**No authentication required** (identity carried in the `state` parameter).

OAuth redirect URI for linking a GA4/GSC Google account. Google redirects here after the user grants analytics permissions.

**Note:** This is not called directly by the frontend — it is the OAuth redirect URI registered with Google Cloud Console. The backend links the ConnectedGoogleAccount record and then redirects the user back to the settings page.

response.name: Google Account Link Callback

query.description: Authorization code from Google
State value from /google-account/oauth-url

item.description: Authentication: Google OAuth, email/password login & registration, token refresh, and password management.

item.name: Users

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

Fetch the complete profile of the currently logged-in user.

**Key fields returned:**
- `id`, `email`, `first_name`, `last_name`
- `role` — determines permissions (`super_admin`, `client_admin`, `client_member`)
- `org_name` — organization name if the user belongs to one
- `connected_google_accounts_count` — how many Google accounts are linked
- `profile_image_url`, `company_logo_url`, `company_name`, `job_role`, etc.

**Call this on app load** to hydrate the auth context / user store.

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

**⚠️ Uses `multipart/form-data` — NOT JSON.**

Update the current user's profile. All fields are optional; send only what you want to change.

**Company logo options:**
- `logo_source: 'url'` + `company_logo: '<url string>'` → use an external image URL
- `logo_source: 'upload'` + `company_logo: <file binary>` → upload a new file

**Tip:** Most HTTP clients automatically set the correct `Content-Type: multipart/form-data` boundary when you use form-data body mode — do not set it manually.

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Uses `multipart/form-data`.

Upload a profile photo for the current user.

**Constraints:**
- Max file size: **5 MB**
- Allowed formats: **JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP**

**Returns** the new `profile_image_url` after successful upload.

**Frontend:** Use a `<input type='file'>`, wrap in `FormData`, POST it. Update the displayed avatar with the returned URL.

response.name: Upload Profile Image

formdata.description: Image file — JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP. Max size: 5MB.

request.description:

List all Google accounts linked to the current user's profile.

**Query param:** `include_properties` (boolean, default `false`):
- `false` → returns each account with `properties_count` only
- `true` → returns each account with a `properties` array of property names

**Use this** on the Google Accounts settings page to show linked accounts and their data coverage.

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

Disconnect (unlink) a Google account from the user's profile.

**Returns 204 No Content** on success — no response body.

**Constraint:** The primary account cannot be disconnected if it is the only linked account or if it is used for authentication — a 400 error is returned in that case.

**Path param:** `account_id` — UUID of the ConnectedGoogleAccount to remove

**Requires authentication.** Only available to **email/password users** (not Google-only accounts).

Change the current user's password.

**Returns** a fresh token pair — update `{{authToken}}` after calling this.

**Fields:**
- `current_password` — the user's existing password (must be correct)
- `new_password` — the new password
- `confirm_new_password` — must match `new_password`

response.name: Change Password
Change Password

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

Initiate an email address change. This does **NOT** immediately update the email — it sends a verification link to the **new** address first.

**Flow:**
1. User submits `new_email` + `password`
2. Backend sends a verification email to the new address
3. User clicks the link in that email
4. `/api/users/me/verify-email-change` finalizes the change

**Fields:**
- `new_email` — the desired new email
- `password` — current password (to confirm identity)

response.name: Request Email Change

query.description: Email change verification token

request.description:
**No authentication required** (token carries identity).

Complete the email change by verifying the token sent to the new email address.

This is the target URL embedded in the verification email link. After successful verification:
- The user's email is updated to the new address
- Any pending email change state is cleared

**Query param:** `token` (required) — from the verification email

response.name: Verify Email Change

query.description: Email change verification token

Check if an organization name is available before creating one.

**Frontend usage:** Call this (debounced) as the user types in the org name field to show real-time availability feedback.

request.description:

Submit a request to join an existing organization.

The org admin will see this in their pending requests list and can approve or decline it.

**Field:** `org_id` — UUID of the organization to request to join

response.name: Submit Join Request

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Create a new organization. The requesting user becomes the owner/admin.

**Returns 201** with the created organization data.

**Fields:**
- `name` (required) — org name, must be unique
- `industry` (optional) — industry category

response.name: Create Organization

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Fetch the full details of the organization the current user owns or admins.

Returns org details including member count, pending request count, and plan limits.

response.name: Get My Organization

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Update the current user's organization name and/or industry.

**All fields optional** — send only what needs updating.

**Fields:** `name` (must be unique if changed), `industry`

response.name: Update My Organization

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

List all **active** members of the current user's organization.

Returns each member's profile, role, join date, and the list of properties they can access.

response.name: List Org Members

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Get all users who have requested to join your org but haven't been approved or declined yet.

Each item includes a `membership_id` — use this UUID to approve or decline via the respective endpoints.

response.name: List Pending Join Requests

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Approve a pending organization join request.

After approval, the user becomes an active `client_member` in the organization.

**Path param:** `membership_id` — from the pending-requests list

response.name: Approve Join Request

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Decline a pending organization join request.

The requesting user will not be added to the organization.

**Path param:** `membership_id` — from the pending-requests list

response.name: Decline Join Request

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Remove an active member from the organization.

The removed user immediately loses access to all organization properties.

**Path param:** `target_user_id` — UUID of the member to remove

response.name: Remove Member

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Invite someone to join the organization by email.

**What happens:**
- If a user with this email already exists → added directly as a member
- Otherwise → an invitation email is sent with a registration link

**Fields:**
- `email` (required) — email to invite
- `full_name` (optional) — pre-fills the name on the registration form
- `property_ids` (required) — UUIDs of properties the new member should access

response.name: Invite Member

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Role: `client_admin` or above.

Replace **all** property access grants for a team member in one call.

**This is a full replace** — send the complete list of property UUIDs the member should access. Any existing access not in this list will be revoked.

**Fields:**
- `property_ids` — array of property UUIDs (send `[]` to revoke all access)

**Path param:** `member_user_id` — UUID of the member to update

response.name: Update Member Property Access

request.description:
**Requires authentication.**

List all properties the current user can access.

**Role-based behaviour:**
- `client_admin` → all properties in their organization
- `client_member` → only properties they have been granted access to
- `super_admin` → all properties; optionally filter by `org_id`

**Each property includes:** `id`, `name`, `ga4_property_id`, `gsc_site_url`, `property_type`, `org_name`, `google_account_email`, `is_favourite`, `is_active`

**Requires authentication.**

Create a new property to track with GA4 and/or Google Search Console data.

**Fields:**
- `name` (required) — friendly display name
- `ga4_property_id` (optional) — GA4 property in format `properties/XXXXXXXXX`. Get valid values from `GET /api/properties/google/properties`.

**Returns 201** with the new property.

Custom comparison end (YYYY-MM-DD)
true to bypass cache and pull fresh data from Google

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Must be property owner or granted member access.

Fetch combined GA4 + GSC analytics dashboard data for the requested date range.

**Date range logic:**
- No dates provided → defaults to the **current calendar month**
- `start_date` + `end_date` → explicit reporting range

**Comparison period:**
- Auto-calculated as the same duration one period prior (default)
- Pass `compare_start_date` + `compare_end_date` for a custom comparison range (disables caching for this request)

**`force_sync=true`** bypasses the cache and fetches fresh data directly from Google APIs.

**Date format:** `YYYY-MM-DD`

**Returns:** Sessions, users, pageviews, bounce rate, top pages, keyword rankings, and comparison deltas.

response.name: Get Dashboard Data

**Date range logic:**

- `start_date` + `end_date` → request analysis for a specific period

**Returns** structured insights: trends, anomalies, opportunities, and action recommendations.

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Must be property owner or granted member access.

Generate a public shareable link to the property's analytics report. The link requires no login — anyone with it can view the report.

**Query param:**
- `expires_in_days` (default `30`, min `1`, max `365`) — how long the link stays active

**Returns** the full `share_url` and the `share_token` (which forms the URL path), plus the `expires_at` timestamp.

request.description:
**Requires authentication.** Must be property owner or granted member access.

Revoke the existing public share link for this property.

After revoking, anyone visiting the old link receives a 404. A new link can be generated at any time via `POST /{property_id}/share`.

Get a paginated list of all users in the system.

**Query params (all optional):**
- `search` — filter by name or email (partial match)
- `role` — filter to a specific role
- `is_suspended` — `true` or `false` to filter by suspension status
- `page` — page number (default `1`)
- `page_size` — users per page (default `20`)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Create a new user with any role (including `super_admin`).

**Fields:**
- `email` (required)
- `first_name`, `last_name` (optional)
- `role` (optional, default `client_admin`)
- `password` (optional — if absent, user can only log in via Google OAuth or forgot-password)
- `org_id` (optional) — assign directly to an organization

response.name: Create User (Admin)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Get full details for a specific user including:
- Profile + organization membership
- Properties they own or have access to
- Login history

**Path param:** `user_id` — UUID of the user

response.name: Get User Details (Admin)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Update a user's profile, role, or organization.

**All fields optional:**
- `first_name`, `last_name` — update name
- `email` — change email (no verification step when done by admin)
- `role` — change the user's role
- `org_id` — move to a different org (`null` to remove from org)

**Path param:** `user_id`

response.name: Update User (Admin)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Suspend a user account. A suspended user cannot log in or access any API endpoint.

Existing sessions are immediately invalidated.

**Path param:** `user_id`

response.name: Suspend User

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Reactivate a previously suspended user account. The user can log in again immediately.

**Path param:** `user_id`

response.name: Activate User

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Get a list of all organizations in the system with member counts and property counts.

response.name: List Organizations (Admin)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Get full details for a specific organization including members, properties, and plan limits.

**Path param:** `org_id` — UUID of the organization

response.name: Get Organization (Admin)

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Get all available user roles in the system.

**Use this** to populate role dropdowns in the admin UI.

response.name: List Roles

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Retrieve the system audit log — a chronological record of significant actions (logins, role changes, suspensions, org modifications, etc.).

response.name: Get Audit Logs

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Create a referral/promo code that organizations can redeem at registration to get specific plan limits.

**Fields:**
- `code` (required) — unique code string (e.g. `STARTUP2025`)
- `max_properties` (optional) — property limit for redeemers
- `duration_months` (optional) — how long promo limits apply
- `max_members` (required) — org member limit for redeemers
- `max_redemptions` (required) — max times the code can be used
- `expiry_date` (optional) — date after which the code is rejected

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Generate a short-lived **impersonation JWT** to act as the target user.

**What it returns:** An `impersonation_token` — use this as the Bearer token to make API calls as that user. This is NOT a regular access token.

**To end:** Call `POST /api/admin/impersonate/end` with the **impersonation token** as Bearer.

**Field:** `reason` (optional) — recorded in the impersonation audit log for accountability.

**Path param:** `target_user_id` — UUID of the user to impersonate

response.name: Impersonate User

request.description:
**Requires the impersonation JWT** (NOT your normal admin Bearer token).

End an active impersonation session and receive back your normal admin tokens.

**How to use:**
1. Get an impersonation token from `POST /api/admin/impersonate/{user_id}`
2. Use it as Bearer to make calls as the target user
3. When done, call this endpoint with the **impersonation token** as Bearer
4. Update `{{authToken}}` to the returned `access_token` to resume your admin session

**Tip:** Store the impersonation token in a separate `{{impersonationToken}}` variable to avoid overwriting your admin token.

response.name: End Impersonation

request.description:
**Requires `super_admin` role.**

Get an audit trail of all impersonation sessions — who impersonated whom, when, for what reason, and for how long.

item.description: Super admin: user management, org admin, referral codes, audit logs, and impersonation.

item.name: Public

request.description:
**No authentication required.** Accessible to anyone with the share link.

Fetch the public analytics dashboard for a property using its share token.

**Embed this in a client-facing page** to show a read-only analytics report. The share token is the last path segment of the URL generated by `POST /{property_id}/share`.

**Path param:** `share_token` — the token from the share URL

Returns a subset of the dashboard data (no sensitive user info).
